title: Senior Embedded Network Software Engineer
description:
Job Title: Senior Embedded Network Software Engineer
   

  

**Location: Remote**
**Job Type:** 
 Full\\-time
   

  

 Position Overview: The Role
   

  

 We are looking for a seasoned
 **Embedded Network Software Engineer** 
 to own the design and optimization of the data plane that powers our entire product portfolio. In this high\\-impact role, you will move beyond simple configuration and dive deep into the internals of high\\-speed packet processing. You will be the go\\-to expert for
 **VPP (Vector Packet Processing)** 
 and
 **DPDK (Data Plane Development Kit)** 
 , responsible for squeezing every ounce of performance out of our hardware while ensuring feature richness and security.
   

  

 This is a hands\\-on architecture and development role. You will be expected to not only write high\\-quality C/C\\+\\+ code but also to define the technical roadmap for the data plane, mentor junior engineers, and collaborate with hardware architects on future designs. Your work will directly impact the throughput, latency, and reliability of our appliances in the most demanding enterprise environments.
   

  

 Key Responsibilities: A Detailed Breakdown
   

  

* Core Data Plane Architecture \\& Development (50% of time)
* VPP Graph Node Development: Design and implement custom VPP graph nodes to introduce new networking features or optimize existing packet processing pipelines. You will need to understand the VPP infrastructure, buffer management (vlib\\_buffer\\_t), and the graph node scheduling framework.
* DPDK Integration \\& Optimization: Manage the integration of DPDK with our software stack. This includes configuring poll\\-mode drivers (PMDs), optimizing the use of hugepages for memory efficiency, managing CPU core affinities (isolcpus), and tuning the system for zero\\-copy packet forwarding.
* Control\\-Plane/Data\\-Plane Interaction: Architect the communication channel between the application control plane (e.g., a Go/Python management daemon) and the high\\-speed VPP/DPDK data plane. This often involves designing shared memory interfaces (memif) or binary APIs to ensure dynamic configuration without disrupting packet flow.
* Feature Implementation: Independently design and deliver complex L2\\-L4 features directly into the data plane, including:
+ L3 Forwarding: Implementing optimized FIB/RIB lookups, ECMP.
+ NAT: Scaling Network Address Translation (SNAT/DNAT) to handle millions of concurrent sessions.
+ Firewalling: Stateful firewall rule processing within the fast path.
+ Tunneling: Encapsulation/decapsulation of VXLAN, GRE, GENEVE, and MPLS over UDP.

* System Performance \\& Benchmarking (20% of time)
* Traffic Generation \\& Validation: Design sophisticated test scenarios using TRex (or similar tools like Ostinato) to simulate real\\-world traffic patterns. You will analyze latency (jitter), throughput (pps/bps), and packet drop rates.
* Bottleneck Analysis: Utilize profiling tools (perf, gprof, VTune) to identify performance bottlenecks, whether they are in the code (e.g., cache misses), the hardware (PCIe bandwidth), or the system configuration (NUMA alignment).
* Optimization Cycles: Propose and implement optimizations, such as vectorizing code, reducing memory copies, or implementing smarter locking mechanisms (RCU), to achieve line\\-rate performance.
* Security \\& Compliance (15% of time)
* Secure Protocol Implementation: Implement and maintain cryptographic data plane features, particularly IPsec (ESP/AH, IKEv2 integration) and TLS termination within the fast path.
* Security Hardening: Conduct threat modeling for new features, ensuring the data plane is resilient to DoS attacks, packet\\-of\\-death scenarios, and other network\\-based exploits. You will implement ACLs and rate\\-limiting directly in the data plane.
* Integration \\& Modernization (15% of time)
* Containerization: Package data plane components (VPP agents) as lightweight Docker containers.
* Orchestration Integration: Work with the DevOps/platform team to integrate our networking stack with Kubernetes. This involves implementing CNI (Container Network Interface) plugins or utilizing the VPP\\-based data plane for service mesh sidecars (e.g., in Istio/Envoy integrations).
* CI/CD for Networking: Define and maintain CI/CD pipelines specifically for testing networking code, ensuring that every commit passes rigorous performance and integration tests.


**Qualifications \\& Experience**
 Qualifications \\& ExperienceBasic Qualifications (Must\\-Have)
   

  

* Education: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, or a related field.
* Total Experience: 8\\+ years of professional experience in software engineering, specifically focused on building and delivering enterprise\\-grade networking appliances (firewalls, routers, load balancers, SmartNICs) or similar complex, high\\-performance systems.
* Technical Leadership: A proven track record of delivering complex features independently for at least 3\\+ years. You must be able to take a vague requirement, architect a solution, write the code, and test it with minimal supervision.
* Programming Languages:
+ C (Expert): Deep understanding of pointers, memory management (malloc/free, arenas), macro usage, and POSIX APIs.
+ C\\+\\+ (Expert): Modern C\\+\\+ (11/14/17\\) experience is required, particularly for control\\-plane components that interface with the data plane. Understanding of RAII, smart pointers, and STL containers is essential.
+ Python (Strong Plus): Ability to write scripts for automation, testing, and data analysis.


 Required Technical Expertise (Granular Breakdown)
   

  

* Data Plane Stack (VPP \\& DPDK)
* VPP (Vector Packet Processing):
+ Must have shipped a product or feature using VPP.
+ Experience writing custom VPP plugins, not just using pre\\-built ones.
+ Deep understanding of the VPP graph architecture and how to add/modify graph nodes.
+ Familiarity with the VPP binary API (e.g., using vppapigen) and the vat (VPP API Tracer) or vppctl for debugging.

* DPDK (Data Plane Development Kit):
+ Must have hands\\-on experience with DPDK versions 19\\.11\\+.
+ In\\-depth knowledge of Environment Abstraction Layer (EAL).
+ Experience with NIC hardware offloads (RSS, checksum offloading, TSO/LRO) and how to enable/configure them via DPDK.
+ Understanding of multi\\-process support in DPDK (primary/secondary processes).
+ Experience with memory pools (mempool) and ring buffers (rte\\_ring) for lockless inter\\-core communication.

* Traffic Generation \\& Testing
* TRex: Strong understanding of the TRex stateful (ASTF) and stateless modes.
* Experience writing complex TRex YAML profiles or using the TRex Python API to generate realistic traffic mixes (HTTP, VoIP, video streaming).
* Ability to analyze TRex latency/jitter histograms to validate QoS guarantees.
* Networking Core (L2\\-L4 Protocols)
* L2: Deep understanding of ARP, MAC learning, VLANs (802\\.1Q), Link Aggregation (LACP).
* L3: Expert knowledge of IPv4 and IPv6\\. Understanding of routing protocols (BGP, OSPF) is a plus, but you must understand the fundamentals of route lookup and FIB management.
* L4: Mastery of TCP state machine, congestion control (its impact on performance), UDP, and ICMP.
* Security \\& Tunneling
* IPsec: Experience with IPsec modes (tunnel/transport), authentication algorithms (SHA1/256\\), encryption algorithms (AES\\-CBC/GCM), and key management (manual vs. IKEv2\\). Experience integrating IPsec with VPP/DPDK is highly desired.
* TLS/SSL: Experience with libraries like OpenSSL or mbedTLS, specifically regarding performance (ASYNC jobs) and integration with non\\-blocking I/O loops.
* Overlays: Hands\\-on experience with VXLAN (including VXLAN\\-GPE) and GRE.
* Technical Stack \\& Modern Tools
* Operating Systems: Expert\\-level Linux knowledge (kernel networking stack comparison, sysctl tuning, NUMA architecture, UIO/VFIO drivers).
* Containerization: Docker (must\\-have). You must know how to write efficient Dockerfiles for embedded environments and handle networking complexities (e.g., giving containers access to host NICs via SR\\-IOV or macvlan).
* Orchestration: Strong preference for experience with Kubernetes. Understanding of Pod networking, Services, Network Policies, and the CNI specification.
* Build \\& Version Control: Git (advanced usage: rebasing, submodules). Experience with build systems like CMake, Meson (used by VPP), or Autotools.


**Nice\\-to\\-Have Skills (Preferred Qualifications)** 
  

  

* Cloud Provider Experience: Proven experience deploying or integrating networking solutions with major public clouds (AWS, Azure, GCP, OCI, Alibaba). Understanding of their proprietary networking constructs (e.g., AWS VPC, Azure VNET).
* Cloud\\-Native Architecture: Familiarity with the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) landscape. Experience with service mesh technologies (Istio/Linkerd) and how they interact with the underlying node networking.
* DevOps \\& Automation:
+ Experience writing CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins).
+ Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, Ansible, Pulumi).
+ Monitoring and observability stacks (Prometheus, Grafana, OpenTelemetry) for network telemetry data.

* Distributed Systems: Strong understanding of distributed system challenges (consistency, fault tolerance). Experience with configuration databases (etcd, Consul) and control plane design for distributed network functions.
* Cross\\-Domain Thinking: The ability to see the big picture. For example, understanding how a small change in the data plane's memory allocation strategy might impact the orchestration layer's ability to scale up services, or how a new feature in the VPP graph could simplify the application control plane logic.
* Security\\-First Mindset: You don't just bolt on security; you design for it. This means understanding common vulnerabilities in C/C\\+\\+ (buffer overflows, use\\-after\\-free), writing code that is auditable, and designing for least\\-privilege principles.


 Skills: python,vpp,trex,design,dpdk,gcp,c,networking,software,kubernetes,docker,cloud,devops,code,architecture

description:
**A Moving Experience.**
 Do you have a passion for pushing the boundaries of innovation? Are you excited about AI’s potential to improve the human experience? Then come join the ride!
   

  

**Who is Cerence AI?**
 Cerence AI is the global leader in AI for transportation, specialized in building AI and voice\\-powered companions for cars, two\\-wheelers, and more that enable people to focus on what matters most. With over 500 million cars shipped with Cerence technology, we partner with leading automakers (such as Volkswagen, Mercedes, Audi, Toyota and many more), mobility providers, and technology companies to power intuitive, integrated experiences that create safer, more connected, and more enjoyable journeys for drivers and passengers alike.
   

  

**Our Driving Force**
 Our team is dedicated to pushing the boundaries of AI innovation, working around the globe with headquarters in Burlington, Massachusetts, USA and 16 other offices across Europe, Asia, and North America. We bring together diverse backgrounds and varied skill sets with the shared goal of advancing the next generation of transportation user experiences. Our culture is customer\\-centric, collaborative, fast\\-paced, and fun, with continuous opportunities for learning and development to support your career growth.
   

  

 Interested in having a significant impact in a dynamic industry with a high\\-performing global team?
   

  

 The Speech Input Division is looking for an excellent Senior software engineer or Senior developer to join the Data Team. We are responsible for the data backend of speech recognition, NLU and AI models. We need you to help ensuring that our systems are always aware of named entities such as artists, music or movies which are aggregated from multiple sources. A key aspect is to ensure the content is pure, matching what end users speak, and sorted by relevance. You will also work on keeping the data pipelines organized well and performant.
   

  

**Your Impact:**
* Text processing in multiple languages
* Extract content from databases
* CI/CD for data pipelines
* Write web scrapers and spiders
* Development of research tools
* Testing and monitoring of the team’s infrastructure


**What You Bring:**
* Education: MSc / MTech in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent.
* Excellent BSc / BTech candidates can be considered.
* 2\\+ years of experience utilizing Python
* Minimum years of work experience: 5
* Scripting skills in Python, especially with Pandas library
* JavaScript \\& Spark/Pyspark
* Unix/Linux on user level
* Querying large databases in SQL, RDF or similar
* Excellent written and spoken English
* Comfortable working in an international, distributed team
* Elasticsearch \\& Data Visualization(preferred skill)
* Experience/Knowledge with cloud computing infrastructure (preferred skill)
* Experience in text data parsing and data mining (preferred skill)
* Experience with data versioning tools such as GIT, DVC (preferred skill)
* Knowledge of orchestration frameworks such as Airflow (preferred skill)

* Background in natural language understanding, data science, or speech recognition (preferred skill)
* Knowledge of multiple languages is a plus, Indian languages as well as international (preferred skill)


**What We Offer**

* Annual bonus opportunity
* Insurance coverage (medical, life, and disability)

* Paid holidays
* Company contribution to the RRSP (Registered Retirement Savings Plan)

* Remote and/or hybrid work available depending on the position
* All compensation and benefits are subject to the terms and conditions of the underlying plans or programs, as applicable, and may be amended, terminated, or replaced from time to time.


 Cerence Inc. (Nasdaq: CRNC and www.cerence.com) is the global industry leader in creating unique, moving experiences for the automotive world. Its track record is built on more than 20 years of industry experience and leadership and more than 500 million cars on the road today across more than 70 languages.

title: Lead Engineer, CPQ
description:
*Join an industry leader and make a positive change in the sustainable use of the world’s natural resources. Together, we will transform the business and drive the industry toward a greener future.*
*At Metso, you will be supported by our inclusive culture and a network of colleagues from around the world. With us, you will embark on a personal growth journey and are encouraged to realize your potential. This is your invitation to rise above the possible.*
 Job posting end date: 03/13/2026
   

  

**Introduction**
 We are seeking a Lead Engineer – CPQ to join our Screening Business Line.
   

  

 In this role, you will lead the end\\-to\\-end solution for adding new screens and feeders to configutor thereby promoting sales through Dealer managment.
   

  

 You will also play a key role in integrating new equipment from acquisitions, planning periodic maintenance of the existing equipment range, and driving continuous process improvements to enhance the digital experience for internal and external users.
   

  

 You will collaborate closely with Product Management, Global Engineering, Regional Engineering, Sales, Order Office, and IT to deliver scalable, reliable CPQ capabilities supporting both capital equipment and aftermarket initiatives.
   

  

 Job Location \\-Alwar
   

  

**Team, you belong to**
 You will join the Global Team dedicated to the Screening Business Line. The team drives CPQ needs for the Screening Business Line across the capital range of equipment, and also supports eCommerce and media configurator initiatives to promote aftermarket sales.
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
**CPQ Solution Leadership (End\\-to\\-End Ownership)**
* Lead the design, development, and deployment of CPQ capabilities for the Screening Business Line.
* Own the configuration model for screens, feeders, and related options (BOM logic, compatibility rules, constraints, variants).
* Ensure CPQ solution quality across configuration accuracy, pricing integrity, quote generation, and order readiness.


**New Product Introduction \\& Catalog Expansion**
* Drive onboarding of new product lines, screens, feeders, and variants into the configurator.
* Partner with engineering and product teams to translate product structures into CPQ rules, attributes, and logical models.
* Support expansion of CPQ catalog for acquired equipment ranges, aligning data structures, standardization, and governance.


**Maintenance \\& Lifecycle Management**
* Plan and execute periodic maintenance for existing equipment range—rules tuning, product retirements, replacements, updates.
* Improve resilience and reduce configuration errors through rule simplification, modularization, and reusable logic components.
* Manage CPQ change requests, release cycles, regression testing, and documentation.


**Process Improvement \\& Digital Experience**
* Identify and implement continuous process improvements for dealers, sales teams, and order office users.
* Optimize the end\\-user journey: intuitive configuration flows, reduced quote time, fewer manual interventions.
* Establish measurable KPIs (e.g., quote cycle time, error rate, adoption, conversion).


**Cross\\-Functional Collaboration \\& Governance**
* Work closely with Sales, Dealer Management, Order Office, and Regional/Global Engineering to align CPQ outputs with business needs.
* Coordinate with IT and enterprise systems teams on integrations (ERP, PLM, CRM), data quality, and security.
* Serve as the CPQ technical and functional SME for screening equipment across stakeholder groups.


**Relationships:**
 You will collaborate with:
   

  

  

* Product Management and Business Owners (Screening Business Line)
* Global Engineering and Regional Engineering teams
* Sales teams and Dealer Management stakeholders
* Order Office / Order Management and Commercial Operations teams
* IT teams (CPQ platform, integrations, data, security)
* E\\-commerce and Media Configurator teams to enable aftermarket sales
* Acquisition integration stakeholders to onboard newly added equipment ranges
* Master Data / Pricing / Finance teams to ensure pricing and product data accuracy


**Who You Are**
* Bachelor’s degree in engineering / computer science / information systems (or equivalent experience).
* Strong experience delivering CPQ solutions for complex products (capital equipment preferred).
* Proven ability to translate product structures into rules, constraints, attributes, pricing logic, and quote outputs.
* Experience working with cross\\-functional stakeholders: product, engineering, sales, operations, and IT.
* Strong analytical problem\\-solving skills and structured approach to troubleshooting complex rule/data issues.
* Excellent communication skills with ability to influence and drive alignment across global teams.


**Preferred Qualifications**
* Hands\\-on experience with CPQ platforms such as SAP CPQ, Salesforce CPQ, Oracle CPQ, Siemens CPQ, Tacton, or similar.
* Experience integrating CPQ with ERP/CRM/PLM (e.g., SAP, Salesforce, Dynamics, Teamcenter, Windchill, etc.).
* Understanding of equipment/product configuration concepts: BOM, variants, options, constraints, commercial rules.
* Experience supporting eCommerce configurators and media configurator initiatives for aftermarket sales.
* Exposure to acquisition integration work (catalog rationalization, data mapping, harmonization).


**What's In It For You**
* Wellbeing and safety \\- benefit from occupational healthcare, generous benefits plan, healthy living rewards, mental well\\-being services, on\\-site gym, meal benefits, car/bicycle benefits, and engagement surveys.
* Annual bonus \\- global incentive programs tied to business and performance targets.
* Extensive learning opportunities \\- ongoing growth dialogues, internal mobility, mentoring programs, education assistance, ambitious projects, and global opportunities.
* Worldwide support \\- leverage our network of peers across the world, offering valuable assistance. We get things done together, through open and honest communication.
* A thriving culture – we are committed to developing an inclusive culture that enables everyone to do their best and reach their full potential. A culture that is courageous, compelling, and caring, and unites our people to build a sustainable future together.


 Hiring Manager: Santhosh Kumar P K

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, religion, color, nationality, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, status as a protected veteran or status as a qualified individual with a disability.*
